channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Linda McMahon defends dismantling the Education Department, shifting its work
item.description: The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.
item.content:encoded:
The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.

(Image credit: Eric Lee)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Cuba's power grid collapses and plunges eastern provinces into a major blackout
item.description: Cuba's aging power grid has eroded in recent years as it faces a prolonged economic crisis, made worse by a U.S. energy blockade of the island.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba's aging power grid has eroded in recent years as it faces a prolonged economic crisis, made worse by a U.S. energy blockade of the island.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Jury orders Boeing to pay $49.5 million to family of 737 MAX crash victim
item.description: A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.
item.content:encoded:
A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.

(Image credit: Stumo Family)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: As Trump meets with Xi, security expert says China now faces the U.S. as a peer
item.description: Former national security official Rush Doshi says President Trump's sky-high tariffs on Chinese goods sparked a clash in which China prevailed. We look at the current state of U.S.-China relations.
item.content:encoded: Former national security official Rush Doshi says President Trump's sky-high tariffs on Chinese goods sparked a clash in which China prevailed. We look at the current state of U.S.-China relations.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Researchers unearth Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur
item.description: They're calling it the "last titan" of Thailand. The sauropod — an herbivore with a long neck and tail — comes from the late Early Cretaceous period, some 100 to 120 million years ago.
item.content:encoded:
They're calling it the "last titan" of Thailand. The sauropod — an herbivore with a long neck and tail — comes from the late Early Cretaceous period, some 100 to 120 million years ago.

(Image credit: Patchanop Boonsai)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: A Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate
item.description: A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.
item.content:encoded:
A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.

(Image credit: Aaron Favila)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: An LA-area mayor acted as an agent for China. Experts say it's part of a pattern
item.description: Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.
item.content:encoded:
Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.

(Image credit: Frazer Harrison)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: Trump tries to make deals in China. And, Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed leader
item.description: President Trump is vying to make deals with China during his visit with President Xi Jinping. And, the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump is vying to make deals with China during his visit with President Xi Jinping. And, the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Cordial Trump-Xi meeting is a 'good sign,' says longtime U.S. diplomat
item.description: Longtime U.S. diplomat Richard Haass interprets President Trump's meeting with China leader, Xi Jinping. He said though the two have different priorities the cordial summit could be a 'good sign.'
item.content:encoded:
Longtime U.S. diplomat Richard Haass interprets President Trump's meeting with China leader, Xi Jinping. He said though the two have different priorities the cordial summit could be a 'good sign.'

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: China's leader warns Trump that differences over Taiwan could lead to a clash
item.description: At the summit, the Chinese leader placed Taiwan, a key point of friction between the U.S. and China, at the center, calling it "the most important issue" between the two countries.
item.content:encoded:
At the summit, the Chinese leader placed Taiwan, a key point of friction between the U.S. and China, at the center, calling it "the most important issue" between the two countries.

(Image credit: China pool)
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby
